description: Forced-Choice triplet blocks for Thurstonian IRT scoring. 100 blocks across all 10 HEXACO domain combinations (10 blocks per combo). Rank-order response: assign 1st, 2nd, 3rd to statements A, B, C. Scoring: first choice +2, second +1, third +0 per domain.
instructions: For each block, rank the three statements from MOST like you (1st) to LEAST like you (3rd). Every statement must receive a unique rank.

A.text: I stay honest about my opinions even when it costs me social approval.

B.text: I feel anxious about negative outcomes even when things are currently going smoothly.

C.text: I am at ease introducing myself to strangers in social situations.

A.text: I wouldn't accept a reward I hadn't earned, even if it were offered freely.

B.text: I feel genuinely moved when someone close to me is going through a hard time.

C.text: People tend to describe me as lively and full of energy.

A.text: I rarely find myself coveting expensive things or the wealth of others.

B.text: I think carefully about the physical risks involved before doing something dangerous.

C.text: I enjoy being around people and seek out social situations when I can.

A.text: I don't think I'm more important or impressive than others.

B.text: When things get really difficult, I need the support of someone I trust.

C.text: I generally feel good about how I come across to others.

A.text: I never pretend to agree with someone just to avoid conflict.

B.text: Worry about future problems is something I live with on a daily basis.

C.text: Public speaking or presenting doesn't make me particularly nervous.

A.text: I follow rules even when breaking them would benefit me and go unnoticed.

B.text: I deeply care about what happens to the people I love.

C.text: I find social gatherings energizing rather than draining.

A.text: Accumulating wealth or luxury is not a priority in my life.

B.text: I am more risk-averse about physical danger than most of my peers.

C.text: I tend to be the most enthusiastic person in the room.

A.text: I am quick to deflect when people praise me too much.

B.text: Facing hard situations alone is more difficult for me than for most.

C.text: I feel at ease in social settings and rarely feel judged.

A.text: I express my real views even in groups where my opinion is the minority.

B.text: I spend a noticeable amount of mental energy anticipating what could go wrong.

C.text: I volunteer to lead discussions or take the floor without much hesitation.

A.text: I wouldn't exaggerate my own contributions when telling others about my work.

B.text: I am the kind of person who cries or feels choked up over others' emotional moments.

C.text: I bring a lot of positive energy when I walk into a room.

A.text: I hold myself to the same ethical standards I expect from others.

B.text: I remain patient with people who are slow or make repeated mistakes.

C.text: I keep my living and working spaces neat and organized.

A.text: Material possessions and wealth don't hold much appeal for me.

B.text: When someone apologizes, I find it easier than most to let things go.

C.text: I maintain my effort and focus on tasks even when they get tedious.

A.text: I don't change what I say based on who I'm talking to.

B.text: I soften criticism so it doesn't feel harsh, even when honesty is needed.

C.text: I notice small errors in my own work and feel compelled to fix them.

A.text: I genuinely don't see myself as more deserving than anyone else.

B.text: I update my views readily when someone gives me a good argument.

C.text: I plan carefully before acting and rarely make impulsive decisions.

A.text: I wouldn't keep a mistake in my favor that benefits me at someone else's expense.

B.text: Long waits or slow processes don't tend to irritate me.

C.text: A tidy environment helps me think clearly and work well.

A.text: I don't feel a strong pull toward social status or expensive things.

B.text: I would rather let a conflict go than hold a grudge against someone.

C.text: When I commit to a task, I see it through even if it becomes harder than expected.

A.text: I say what I mean, not what people want to hear from me.

B.text: I go out of my way to avoid saying things that might wound someone's feelings.

C.text: I set high standards for my own output and feel unsatisfied when they aren't met.

A.text: I feel awkward receiving a lot of praise and try to redirect attention.

B.text: I can hold two opposing viewpoints in my head without becoming defensive.

C.text: I think about the long-term consequences before I make significant decisions.

A.text: I would correct an error in my favor even if no one else would ever notice it.

B.text: I stay composed when someone else's slowness or error affects my plans.

C.text: I create systems to keep track of my work and belongings reliably.

A.text: I'm not really motivated by the idea of becoming rich or famous.

B.text: I can forgive someone who has genuinely hurt me if they show real remorse.

C.text: I work just as hard when no one is monitoring my progress as when they are.

B.text: I often find myself seeking out the company of others in my free time.

C.text: I am drawn to philosophical questions and abstract ideas even when they aren't practical.

C.text: I tend to generate novel ideas or unusual solutions to problems.
